seniority.level: Manager / Senior IC (likely equivalent to L5-L6)
level_signals: Title is 'Manager' but JD describes IC-style ownership with cross-functional influence, not direct reports
Comp range $136K-$200K base nationally, consistent with senior IC or manager-level at DoorDash
Expected to influence senior teammates who don't report to you

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of relevant experience in high-performance cultures (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, or a related experience)
You solve problems from first principles. You rethink problems from the ground up, and resist "this is how it's always been done" thinking.
You are a builder at heart, and have already adopted AI tools beyond ChatGPT to meaningfully improve how you work
You thrive leading cross-functional teams. You can empathize with diverse perspectives, chart the right path, and influence senior teammates who don't report to you
You're highly analytical and find the signal through the noise. You are equally at home picking apart funnel metrics as you are pulling insights from 50+ sales calls

You're equally comfortable operating at 10,000 feet and 1 foot. You don't hesitate to get in the weeds and operate at the lowest level of detail, but you're just as comfortable thinking long-term and inspiring a team. No job is beneath you.

The ideal candidate for this role should be a self-starter with an entrepreneurial mindset, able to take ownership and create plans from scratch, and drive execution while obtaining cross-functional buy-in

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: High-growth SaaS experience is nice-to-have, but not required

scope.surface: Digital Ordering SaaS business unit — online ordering, branded mobile apps, loyalty solutions for merchants (restaurants, grocers, retailers)
scope.business_model: SaaS (first-party digital ordering platform sold to merchants)
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue growth across Digital Ordering segment
core_responsibilities: Drive high visibility, cross-functional workstreams addressing Digital Ordering's most challenging questions
Shape strategies, workstreams, and playbooks across go-to-market priorities
Design and influence GTM sales motion at each stage of the sales funnel
Develop deep understanding of go-to-market engine and use it to scale
Parse complex data sets and qualitative feedback to provide insights and recommendations
